eiri.line: Bartók's Music for Strings, Percussion and Celesta. Kubrick used it in 2001 too. He found something in it that keeps working.

line: The manager is talking.
Jack isn't listening. His eyes are somewhere else the whole time.
Steadicam was new in 1980. Garrett Brown invented it.
That floating quality isn't supernatural — it's mechanical.

eiri.line: Mr. Orange is lying in the blood. He's there for the whole film.

line: The carpet pattern is a Navajo design.
Kubrick had it custom-made. It doesn't match any real Overlook.
Danny's tricycle routes were mapped obsessively.
The Steadicam follows at floor level — his height, not ours.
Room 217 in the novel.
Kubrick changed it to 237 because the Timberline Lodge actually had a room 217 and didn't want guests to avoid it.
The moon is 237,000 miles from Earth.
Kubrick probably didn't intend that. He probably did.
The hedge maze didn't exist in the original hotel.
Kubrick replaced the topiary animals from the novel — he thought moving bushes would look ridiculous.
Shelley Duvall filmed the baseball bat scene 127 times.
She had a breakdown during production.
Kubrick documented it.

eiri.line: Wendy is reading The Catcher in the Rye while Jack types. He's writing. She's reading about someone else's unraveling.

line: Midnight, the Stars and You. Ray Noble, 1934.
Playing in the Gold Room, which shouldn't exist.
The song predates the hotel.

eiri.line: The Gold Room bar is always fully stocked and always empty. Lloyd appears before anyone opens the door.

line: Here and there the camera can't possibly be where it is.
A window appears in a room that shouldn't have one.
Kubrick left them in.
Penderecki, De Natura Sonoris No. 2.
The voices aren't singing words — they're functioning as texture.
The choir is the room itself.
Wendy never goes looking for what Jack is writing. She waits.
Then she sees it.
That's the whole marriage.
All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy.
Every line formatted differently — different margins, different spacing.
The blood from the elevator was vegetable-based.
Kubrick tested nine different shades.
The set had to be fully cleaned between takes. That shot took a year.

eiri.line: Here's Johnny was improvised. Nicholson remembered Ed McMahon's line from The Tonight Show. Kubrick kept it.

line: July 4th, 1921.
He's standing in the center.
He's always been the caretaker.
